key_strengths: 91% gross margins with $684M in free cash flow and near-zero capex
Fortress balance sheet: zero long-term debt, $2.5B cash, 12x current ratio
Unique 20-year non-replicable human-content corpus central to AI training pipelines
Strong network effects across 100,000+ self-sustaining subreddits
AI data licensing revenue stream (~$140M in 2025) with major contracts with Google and OpenAI
key_risks: No margin of safety at ~$135 — bear-case DCF (~$78) is 42% below current price
SBC at 18% of revenue ($387M) materially overstates shareholder economics; true P/E closer to 67-77x
Active FTC investigation and EU AI Act enforcement exposure threaten data licensing stream
Platform dependency on Google for new user discovery creates single-point vulnerability
Revenue growth decelerating (70% Q4 2025 → 52-54% guided Q1 2026) at a demanding multiple
red_flags: Only one year of profitability — insufficient capital allocation track record
Dual-class voting structure with no time-based sunset
Bear-case intrinsic value (~$78) is below current trading price (~$135)
Upside-to-downside ratio of only 1.4x ($213 base vs. $78 bear)
buy_triggers: Price declines to $90-110, bringing the stock into base-case margin-of-safety territory
FTC formally clears Reddit's data licensing practices and/or EU AI Act enforcement does not impair Google/OpenAI contracts
Two or three consecutive quarters of US logged-in DAU reacceleration and international ARPU gap beginning to visibly close
sell_triggers: Two consecutive quarters of revenue growth below 40% without an adequate structural explanation
FTC issues a formal complaint or EU enforcement authority issues an order restricting Reddit's data licensing practices
Stock re-rates to $250+ without commensurate earnings improvement (multiple expands back toward 60-70x forward P/E without acceleration)
valuation_summary: Base-case DCF yields ~$213 (implying ~58% upside from ~$135), but the bear-case DCF of ~$78 sits 42% below the current price, and achieving the base case requires sustaining 40-50% revenue CAGR through 2028-2029 with EBITDA margins expanding to 38-42% — achievable but not conservative assumptions. At ~$135, there is no margin of safety; the stock becomes attractive in the $90-110 range where the bear-case downside narrows to 15-29%.
source_summary: Analysis draws on Reddit Q4 2025 earnings release (BusinessWire, Feb 5 2026), Q4 2025 earnings call transcript, full-year 2025 financials via MacroTrends, DemandSage user statistics, GuruFocus, CNBC coverage, and supplementary sources for valuation, balance sheet, and regulatory context.
